abstract: This research explores the implementation and impact of religious character education programs on the daily lives of students at MI Nurul Yaqin Surabaya. Employing a descriptive qualitative approach, the study gathered data through interviews, field observations, and documentation. The findings indicate that the madrasah has successfully established a well-structured and functional religious character education framework, integrated through daily routines such as morning assemblies, Quranic recitation (PBA), and congregational prayers. While current programs are running smoothly, the study identifies several areas for refinement to maximize student engagement and effectiveness. Based on these results, it is recommended that the madrasah conduct systematic evaluations, provide specialized pedagogical training for teachers, and foster stronger partnerships with parents to reinforce religious values within the home environment. By adopting these strategies, MI Nurul Yaqin can ensure its character-building initiatives achieve their intended goals and contribute meaningfully to the holistic moral development of its students.
